classification_evidence: Example changes ADLC framework contracts and local CLI validation.

supported.claim: Implementation-interface contracts and productionization gates are optional Build Brief fields.

supported_claims: New fields are optional and backward compatible.

section_policy.reason: The example demonstrates an active implementation-interface contract.
The example demonstrates an active productionization gate.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: schema or readiness check rejects missing interface or productionization evidence

verification_spec.scope_note: The local CLI validates schema support, payload preservation, and production_ready readiness evidence.

enterprise_readiness_contract.production_grade_target: Demonstrate ADLC-native implementation-interface and productionization gate contracts.
enterprise_readiness_contract.backward_compatibility: Existing Build Briefs remain valid when the optional fields are absent.
enterprise_readiness_contract.forward_compatibility: New work-item emitters can preserve the contracts without changing target-specific clients.
failure_mode_coverage: Readiness blocks production_ready claims without evidence.
enterprise_readiness_contract.compliance_posture: No secrets, external auth, or production mutation is introduced.

sections.1_context: This example shows how ADLC decomposes compound engineering work into an implementation-interface contract and a productionization gate without adding a parallel workflow.
sections.2_scope_and_constraints: In scope: schema-valid example artifact and local CLI readiness. Out of scope: external provider mutation or broad compliance readiness.
sections.3_repo_findings: ADLC already has Build Brief schemas, workflow state, normalized work-item payloads, readiness checks, and emitter contracts that can be extended.
sections.4_architecture: The implementation task carries the interface and productionization fields. The validation task proves schema validation and readiness.
sections.6_observability_slo: The productionization gate captures owner, rollback, runbook, and dashboard refs as evidence, but this example does not add runtime observability.
sections.7_execution_plan: Validate the artifact, emit work items in dry-run mode, and require readiness.

8_task_tickets.title: Preserve implementation-interface and productionization fields
8_task_tickets.objective: Preserve task-level implementation-interface and productionization gate fields through normalized ADLC work-item emission.

decision_contract.resolution: Optional fields extend the current Build Brief contract.

scope: Preserve optional task-level contracts in local emitter payloads.

acceptance_criteria.given: a task includes implementation_interface_contract and productionization_gate
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: jq can read both fields from the emitted artifact

anti_slop_rules: Do not add a separate productionization workflow.
Do not claim external provider readiness from local dry-run evidence.

tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: The example is schema-only and does not mutate external systems.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing tasks without the optional fields emit unchanged.
compatibility_contract.forward: Future emitters can preserve the fields without schema redesign.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: No migration required; optional schema fields only.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: normalized payload drops interface or productionization fields

verification_spec.scope_note: Dry-run emission exercises the local payload and readiness path.

definition_of_done: The emitted implementation task includes both contracts.
failure_modes: Optional fields are dropped by normalization.

minimum_fields.name: coverage_state
minimum_fields.constraint: one of unsupported, evidence_only, monitor_only, not_yet_ga, governed, production_ready

invariants: Optional fields remain optional for older briefs.
Production-ready claims must not bypass readiness.
failure_semantics: Unsupported production_ready claims fail readiness before mutation.
implementation_interface_contract.privacy_redaction: No secrets or external credentials are emitted.

productionization_gate.claim: ADLC can preserve implementation-interface and productionization contracts through local dry-run work-item emission.

operational_readiness.deployment_path: Schema and CLI patch in this repository.
operational_readiness.rollback_path: Revert the schema and CLI patch.

security_privacy.redaction_posture: The local payload contains task metadata and no secrets.

reliability_failure_modes: Readiness mismatch blocks before external mutation.
no_overclaim: This does not prove external Linear, GitHub, or Jira provider mutation.
This does not certify broad compliance readiness.
known_unsupported_states: External tracker API transport is out of scope.

8_task_tickets.title: Validate implementation-interface productionization example
8_task_tickets.objective: Run schema validation and readiness checks for the example Build Brief.

decision_contract.resolution: Validation task owns local evidence capture.

scope: Run local schema and readiness commands.

acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: CLI exits 0 and readiness_report.status is ready

anti_slop_rules: Do not treat dry-run evidence as external provider mutation proof.

compatibility_contract.backward: Older briefs without optional fields keep passing.
compatibility_contract.forward: Future examples can add target-specific evidence without changing the contract.

implementation_notes: Capture command output as validation evidence.

verification_spec.scope_note: The validation task proves the checked-in example is runnable.

failure_modes: Example schema drifts from the contract.
Readiness blocks a valid production_ready example.

implementation_interface_contract.reason: Validation task does not change an integration boundary.

productionization_gate.reason: Validation task does not make a production support claim.

9_risks: Overclaiming dry-run evidence as external provider readiness.
sections.10_rollout: No rollout. The artifact is a checked-in example for local validation.

queries_run: Where should implementation-interface and productionization support be added in ADLC?

17_productionization_gates.claim: Local dry-run emission preserves the contracts.
no_overclaim: External provider mutation is out of scope.
